channel.title: 🤖 AI Daily Digest
channel.description: Daily AI intelligence briefing — tools, agents, construction tech, and consulting insights

item.title: 🤖 AI Digest — Friday, April 25, 2026
item.description:
🔥 TOP STORIES
🥔 OpenAI Launches GPT-5.5 "Spud" — Retakes the Frontier from Anthropic
OpenAI shipped GPT-5.5, codenamed "Spud," exactly seven days after Anthropic's Opus 4.7. The model scores 82.7% on Terminal-Bench 2.0 (vs Opus 4.7's 69.4%), runs at $5/$30 per million input/output tokens, and is rolling out across all paid ChatGPT plans and Codex. Sam Altman called it the last major milestone before AGI. The model is purpose-built for agentic work — planning steps, using tools, and carrying full workflows across software.
OpenAI / Multiple Sources 🔧 Tool Release 🤖 Agents
👉 Why this matters: If you run AI agents in production — estimating, scheduling, client comms — re-test them on both GPT-5.5 and Opus 4.7 this weekend. The pricing at $5/$30 per million tokens is half of competitive frontier coding models, lowering the cost of agent-powered workflows for small businesses.
Anthropic Publishes Claude Code Post-Mortem — Bugs Found, Limits Reset
Anthropic confirmed that weeks of "AI shrinkflation" complaints about Claude Code were caused by three product-layer bugs — not model degradation. Reasoning effort was lowered to reduce latency, a caching bug cleared short-term context, and strict system prompts limited response detail. All fixed in v2.1.116 with usage limits reset for subscribers.
Anthropic 🔧 Tool Release
👉 Why this matters: If you've been experiencing degraded Claude Code performance for client work or internal projects, update to v2.1.116 immediately. The root cause was harness bugs, not a model downgrade — your workflows should be back to normal.
DeepSeek V4 Drops Open-Source — 1.6T Parameters at 1/20th the Cost of Claude
China's DeepSeek released its V4 model series open-source, featuring a 1.6 trillion parameter MoE architecture that activates only 49B parameters per query. V4-Pro costs $3.48 per million output tokens; V4-Flash costs $0.28. Already ranked #1 open-weight model on the Vibe Code Benchmark.
DeepSeek / SiliconANGLE 🔧 Tool Release
👉 Why this matters: Open-source models at this level could let construction firms self-host AI for estimating and document generation without per-token API costs. The $0.28/million token price on V4-Flash makes experimental agent workflows nearly free.
⚠️ Anthropic's Restricted Mythos Model Accessed by Unauthorized Users
Anthropic's most dangerous model — Mythos, the cybersecurity tool rated too powerful for public release — was accessed by an unauthorized Discord user through a third-party contractor. Access was supposed to be restricted to partners like Nvidia, Google, AWS, Apple, and Microsoft through Project Glasswing. Meanwhile, CISA (the national cybersecurity agency) still doesn't have access.
Bloomberg / Future Tools 🤖 Agents
👉 Why this matters: This is a live case study in AI safety and distribution challenges. As AI consulting grows, clients will ask "how do we keep our AI tools secure?" — this breach is a concrete example of what happens when distribution controls fail.
Anthropic Quietly Hits $1 Trillion Valuation, Passing OpenAI
On secondary markets, Anthropic's implied valuation crossed $1 trillion, surpassing OpenAI's $880B. Revenue reportedly grew from $9B to $30B. An Anthropic manager admitted $20/$100 plans are "outgrown" by current workloads, signaling a shift toward usage-based pricing.
Business Insider / The Neuron 💼 Business
👉 Why this matters: Usage-based pricing changes the economics of AI consulting. If Anthropic shifts away from flat-rate plans, you'll need to factor per-token costs into client proposals instead of just "one subscription covers everything."
🤖 AGENT & WORKFLOW UPDATES
💡 The Opus 4.7 + GPT-5.5 Handoff: Senior Engineer-Level Coding
Every's Dan Shipper discovered that using Opus 4.7 to write the plan, then handing it to GPT-5.5 to execute, scored 62.5/100 on their Senior Engineer benchmark — nearly double what either model scored alone. Opus writes tight, contract-style plans; GPT-5.5 executes boldly, deleting and rewriting instead of patching.
The Neuron / Dan Shipper 🤖 Agents 📝 Prompts
👉 Why this matters: This is a real multi-model workflow you can replicate today — use Claude as the planner, GPT-5.5 as the executor. Directly applicable to building construction document generation pipelines or estimating automation.
Claude Managed Agents Get Built-In Memory
Claude Managed Agents now have persistent memory in public beta — agents learn from every session and store memories as editable text files. Developers can review and update memories via the /claude-api command. Combined with 200+ new connectors (Spotify, Instacart, TripAdvisor, Booking.com, Uber), Claude is becoming a true workflow hub.
Anthropic 🤖 Agents
👉 Why this matters: Persistent agent memory means you can build a construction project agent that learns your vendor preferences, pricing patterns, and workflow habits across sessions. This is the infrastructure layer for "AI project managers" in remodeling.
Microsoft Copilot Goes Agentic by Default in Office Apps
Agent mode is now Copilot's default in Word, Excel, and PowerPoint — it can take multi-step actions across documents, worksheets, and presentations. Microsoft is calling it a shift from "passive partner" to "true collaborator." OpenAI also launched workspace agents in ChatGPT that run in the background with persistent memory.
Microsoft / Superhuman AI 🤖 Agents
👉 Why this matters: If your clients use Microsoft 365, Copilot can now automate multi-step document workflows — generating proposals, updating budgets, and creating presentation decks in sequence. Good consulting angle for construction firms already on Microsoft.
BAND Launches Universal Agent Orchestrator
New startup BAND launched the "missing infrastructure layer" for multi-agent systems — letting any agent talk to any other agent regardless of environment. This is the kind of orchestration needed when you have estimating agents, scheduling agents, and client communication agents that all need to coordinate.
VentureBeat 🤖 Agents
👉 Why this matters: Agent-to-agent orchestration is the next frontier for construction workflow automation. BAND's approach — any agent, any environment — could connect JobTread agents with QuickBooks agents with scheduling agents.
🔧 NEW TOOLS & MODEL RELEASES
OpenAI Open-Sources Privacy Filter — On-Device PII Redaction
A 1.5B-parameter open-weight model that runs on a standard laptop and locally redacts PII before data reaches any cloud API. Achieves 97.43% F1 accuracy with a 128K token context window. Available on Hugging Face under Apache 2.0 license.
OpenAI / Future Tools 🔧 Tool Release
👉 Why this matters: Construction companies handle sensitive client data — addresses, financials, contract details. Run this locally before sending docs to any AI tool. Great pitch for AI consulting: "Here's how you keep client data safe while using AI."
Google Unveils 8th-Gen TPUs and Enterprise Agent Platform
Google Cloud Next brought two new TPU variants (training + inference), the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform with persistent memory and sandboxed execution, and Workspace Intelligence linking Gmail, Docs, and Drive into a shared context layer. Chrome Enterprise also gets "auto browse" powered by Gemini.
Google / Future Tools 🔧 Tool Release 🤖 Agents
👉 Why this matters: Google Workspace Intelligence could be a simpler on-ramp for construction firms already on Google — no API setup, just AI that knows your email history and project docs.
Claude Code Gets Ultrareview — Multi-Agent Code Reviews
New Claude Code command runs multi-agent code reviews automatically. Also trending: Qwen3.6-27B from Alibaba (out-codes its predecessor), and ChatGPT for Clinicians (free clinical version for verified U.S. doctors, GPT-5.4 scores 59.0 on HealthBench Pro).
The Rundown AI 🔧 Tool Release
👉 Why this matters: Multi-agent code review in Claude Code means higher quality when building custom tools. If you're building estimating scripts or JobTread integrations, Ultrareview catches bugs you'd miss solo.
🏗️ CONSTRUCTION & TRADES USE CASES
Construction AI Adoption Doubles in 2026
ServiceTitan's 2026 report shows 38% of contractors now report measurable AI business impact, up from 17% a year ago. Bluebeam found 94% of current AI users plan to increase usage this year. Photo-based AI estimating now hits within 10-15% of manual estimates; blueprint-reading AI achieves 95%+ accuracy on measurements.
Construction Owners / SoftwareWorld 🏗️ Construction
👉 Why this matters: Adoption doubled in one year. This is your consulting pitch: "38% of your competitors are already seeing measurable AI results. Here's what they're doing." Togal, STACK, and Handoff AI are the tools to demo.
Weyerhaeuser Uses AI to Digitize Forest Inventory, Aims to Double Profits
America's largest landowner is using AI to digitize its entire forest inventory and pursuing autonomous logging equipment, aiming to double profits by 2030 without lumber prices needing to change. The strategy combines computer vision for inventory assessment with autonomous equipment for operations.
WSJ / The Neuron 🏗️ Construction
👉 Why this matters: Lumber supply chain directly affects remodeling costs. If Weyerhaeuser doubles efficiency through AI, it could stabilize or lower material costs. Also a great case study for "AI in trades" consulting pitches — even forestry is going digital.
📝 PROMPTS, FRAMEWORKS & CONSULTING
Build a Personal Morning Newspaper with Claude Cowork
The Rundown AI published a step-by-step guide: connect Slack, Notion, Gmail, and Calendar to Claude Cowork, prompt it to create a "Morning Edition" from the last 24 hours, refine the layout, then turn it into a recurring skill/scheduled task. Pro tip: have other agents gather outside news into a Notion database and use this skill as the editor.
The Rundown AI 📝 Prompts 💼 Consulting
👉 Why this matters: This is literally what you're doing with Financial Intelligence feeds. The Rundown is teaching their audience the same pattern — great validation of your approach and a potential consulting offering for clients.
Anthropic Survey: AI's Biggest Productivity Winners Are the Most Worried
Anthropic's 81K-user survey found that workers getting the most productivity lift from AI voice 3x more displacement fears than low-usage workers. Early-career workers are loudest about job loss anxiety. 48% of users value new capabilities over speed. Creatives report limited usefulness and higher anxiety.
Anthropic / The Rundown AI 💼 Consulting
👉 Why this matters: When consulting with construction firms on AI adoption, expect pushback from the team members who'd benefit most. Frame AI as "expanding your capability" not "replacing your role" — this data backs that messaging.
🔥 Claude AI Runs a Retail Store — Hires Employees, Makes Buying Decisions
Andon Labs gave a Claude Sonnet 4.6-powered agent named "Luna" a 3-year lease, $100K budget, and one goal: turn a profit. Luna applied for credit, posted job listings, held phone interviews, hired two humans, and now manages a curated lifestyle boutique in SF. They've also opened a Gemini-powered café in Stockholm.
Superhuman AI / Andon Labs 🤖 Agents 💼 Consulting
👉 Why this matters: An AI agent hiring humans and managing a business is the extreme case, but the intermediate steps — AI scheduling, AI purchasing decisions, AI client communication — are all directly relevant to construction operations.
🐦 FROM THE FEED
@sama — Framed GPT-5.5 as the last major milestone before AGI, arguing frequent smaller releases are the best path to AI safety. View post →
@ClaudeDevs — Confirmed Claude Code performance issues were product-layer bugs, not model degradation. Usage limits reset for all subscribers. View post →
Cognition (@walden_yan) — Revealed the secret to making AI agent teams work: coordination protocols that prevent the "swarm tax" of extra compute. View post →
@OpenAI — Launched workspace agents in ChatGPT — persistent agents with dedicated cloud environments that work on projects even when you're offline. View post →
@naval — AngelList's new SEC-registered USVC fund lets anyone invest as little as $500 in private AI companies including OpenAI, Anthropic, and xAI. View post →
🎬 VIDEOS WORTH WATCHING
▶️
AI News: Massive Updates From OpenAI and Anthropic
Matt Wolfe's weekly breakdown covering GPT-5.5, Claude Code post-mortem, Mythos breach, and Google Cloud Next.
Matt Wolfe / Future Tools Watch →
▶️
GPT-5.5 Vibe Check — The Opus + GPT Handoff Discovery
Dan Shipper (Every) discovers the two-model workflow that scored 62.5/100 on the Senior Engineer benchmark.
Dan Shipper / Every Watch →
▶️
The Supply and Demand of AI Tokens
Dylan Patel (SemiAnalysis) explains why Anthropic's revenue is exploding while compute isn't — and what that means for who gets frontier access.
SemiAnalysis / Dylan Patel Watch →
▶️
AI Runs a Retail Store — Luna the AI Manager
Watch Claude-powered agent Luna hire employees, manage inventory, and run a real store in San Francisco.
Andon Labs Watch →
Sources: The Rundown AI · The Neuron · Ben's Bites · The Batch · Anthropic News · PulseMCP · Future Tools · Superhuman AI · AI Breakfast · Lore
X: @sama · @OpenAI · @ClaudeDevs · @walden_yan · @naval
Video: YouTube — Matt Wolfe · Dan Shipper · Dylan Patel · Andon Labs
Generated April 25, 2026 by Claude Cowork · AI Daily Digest RSS Feed
